---
title: "CSS property reference"
format: html
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{CSS property reference}
  %\VignetteEngine{quarto::html}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

This reference documents every CSS property that docstyle maps to Word/OOXML formatting. For a tutorial on creating Word templates from CSS, see [Creating Word templates from CSS](css-to-word.html). For YAML configuration options, see [YAML configuration reference](reference-yaml-schema.html).

## Quick reference

| CSS Property | OOXML Element | Unit | Applies to |
|---|---|---|---|
| `font-family` | `w:rFonts` | — | All selectors |
| `font-size` | `w:sz` | Half-points | All selectors |
| `font-weight` | `w:b` | Boolean | All selectors |
| `font-style` | `w:i` | Boolean | All selectors |
| `color` | `w:color` | Hex (no `#`) | All selectors |
| `text-decoration` | `w:u`, `w:strike` | — | All selectors |
| `text-align` | `w:jc` | — | Paragraph selectors |
| `line-height` | `w:spacing` (line) | 240ths of line | Paragraph selectors |
| `margin-top` | `w:spacing` (before) | Twips | Paragraph selectors |
| `margin-bottom` | `w:spacing` (after) | Twips | Paragraph selectors |
| `margin-left` | `w:ind` (left) | Twips | Paragraph selectors |
| `padding-left` | `w:ind` (left) | Twips | Paragraph selectors |
| `text-indent` | `w:ind` (firstLine/hanging) | Twips | Paragraph selectors |
| `border-*` | `w:tblBorders`, `w:tcBorders` | Eighth-points | Table selectors |
| `background-color` | `w:shd` | Hex | Table `th`/`td` selectors |
| `size` | `w:pgSz` | Twips | `@page` rules |
| `margin` | `w:pgMar` | Twips | `@page` rules |

## Text properties

These CSS properties map to Word run properties (`w:rPr`) and apply to any selector.

### `font-family`

Sets the font for the Word style. Only the first non-generic font in the stack is used; generic families (`serif`, `sans-serif`, `monospace`) are skipped.

```css
p { font-family: "Times New Roman", serif; }
h1 { font-family: "Arial", "Helvetica", sans-serif; }
```

**OOXML output:**

```xml
<w:rFonts w:ascii="Times New Roman" w:hAnsi="Times New Roman"/>
```

The font must be installed on the system where the document is rendered.

### `font-size`

Sets the font size. Converted to half-points for Word (12pt = 24 half-points).

```css
body { font-size: 12pt; }
.footnote-text { font-size: 10pt; }
```

**Supported units:** `pt`, `px` (1px = 0.75pt), `in`, `cm`, `mm`, `pc`, `em`, `rem`, `%`.

**OOXML output:**

```xml
<w:sz w:val="24"/>   <!-- 12pt -->
<w:szCs w:val="24"/> <!-- complex script mirror -->
```

### `font-weight`

Controls bold formatting. Numeric values 600 and above are treated as bold; values below 600 are normal.

```css
h1 { font-weight: bold; }    /* or 700 */
p { font-weight: normal; }   /* or 400 */
```

**OOXML output:**

```xml
<w:b/>         <!-- bold on -->
<w:b w:val="0"/>  <!-- bold explicitly off -->
```

### `font-style`

Controls italic formatting.

```css
blockquote { font-style: italic; }
p { font-style: normal; }
```

**OOXML output:**

```xml
<w:i/>         <!-- italic on -->
<w:i w:val="0"/>  <!-- italic explicitly off -->
```

### `color`

Sets text colour. Accepts hex values, named colours, and `rgb()` notation.

```css
h1 { color: #1a1a2e; }
.abstract { color: #666666; }
```

**Supported formats:**

| Format | Example | Result |
|---|---|---|
| 6-digit hex | `#1a1a2e` | `1A1A2E` |
| 3-digit hex | `#333` | `333333` |
| Named colour | `red` | `FF0000` |
| RGB function | `rgb(26, 26, 46)` | `1A1A2E` |
| `transparent` | `transparent` | `auto` |

**OOXML output:**

```xml
<w:color w:val="1A1A2E"/>
```

### `text-decoration`

Controls underline and strikethrough. Multiple values can be combined.

```css
.emphasis { text-decoration: underline; }
.deleted { text-decoration: line-through; }
.both { text-decoration: underline line-through; }
```

**OOXML output:**

```xml
<w:u w:val="single"/>  <!-- underline -->
<w:strike/>             <!-- strikethrough -->
```

### `font` (shorthand)

The CSS `font` shorthand is expanded to individual properties.

```css
h1 { font: bold 16px "Helvetica", "Arial", sans-serif; }
```

Extracts `font-weight: bold`, `font-size: 16px`, `font-family: "Helvetica"`.

## Paragraph properties

These CSS properties map to Word paragraph properties (`w:pPr`) and apply to paragraph-level selectors (headings, body text, etc.).

### `text-align`

Sets paragraph alignment.

```css
p { text-align: left; }
.title { text-align: center; }
blockquote { text-align: justify; }
```

| CSS value | OOXML value |
|---|---|
| `left` | `left` |
| `right` | `right` |
| `center` | `center` |
| `justify` | `both` |

### `line-height`

Sets line spacing. Only numeric multipliers are supported (not unit-based values like `18pt`).

```css
body { line-height: 1.5; }   /* 1.5× spacing */
p { line-height: 2.0; }      /* double spacing */
```

**Conversion:** multiplier × 240 (Word uses 240ths of a line).

| CSS value | OOXML `w:line` | Spacing |
|---|---|---|
| `1.0` | `240` | Single |
| `1.15` | `276` | Default Word |
| `1.5` | `360` | 1.5 lines |
| `2.0` | `480` | Double |

**OOXML output:**

```xml
<w:spacing w:line="360" w:lineRule="auto"/>
```

### `margin-top` and `margin-bottom`

Set paragraph spacing before and after. Converted to twips (1in = 1440 twips, 1pt = 20 twips).

```css
h1 { margin-top: 24pt; margin-bottom: 6pt; }
p { margin-bottom: 12pt; }
```

**OOXML output:**

```xml
<w:spacing w:before="480" w:after="120"/>  <!-- 24pt, 6pt -->
```

### `margin-left` and `padding-left`

Set paragraph left indentation. Both properties map to the same OOXML attribute.

```css
blockquote { margin-left: 0.5in; }
.bibliography { padding-left: 0.5in; }
```

**OOXML output:**

```xml
<w:ind w:left="720"/>  <!-- 0.5in = 720 twips -->
```

### `text-indent`

Sets first-line or hanging indentation. Positive values indent the first line; negative values create a hanging indent.

```css
.first-paragraph { text-indent: 0.5in; }
.bibliography { text-indent: -0.5in; padding-left: 0.5in; }  /* hanging indent */
```

**OOXML output:**

```xml
<!-- First-line indent -->
<w:ind w:firstLine="720"/>

<!-- Hanging indent (negative text-indent) -->
<w:ind w:hanging="720" w:left="720"/>
```

## Page layout (`@page` rules)

Page layout is configured via CSS `@page` rules, following the CSS Paged Media specification. These properties are read during pre-render and written to the reference.docx template.

### `size`

Sets page dimensions and orientation.

```css
@page { size: letter; }
@page landscape { size: letter landscape; }
@page { size: a4 portrait; }
```

| Value | Dimensions |
|---|---|
| `letter` | 8.5 × 11 in |
| `a4` | 210 × 297 mm |
| `legal` | 8.5 × 14 in |

Append `landscape` or `portrait` for orientation.

### `margin`

Sets page margins. Supports CSS shorthand (1–4 values).

```css
/* All sides */
@page { margin: 1in; }

/* Top/bottom and left/right */
@page { margin: 1in 1.25in; }

/* Top, left/right, bottom */
@page { margin: 1in 1.25in 0.75in; }

/* Top, right, bottom, left */
@page { margin: 1in 1.25in 0.75in 1.5in; }

/* Individual sides */
@page { margin-top: 1in; margin-bottom: 0.75in; }
```

Individual margins (`margin-top`, `margin-right`, `margin-bottom`, `margin-left`) override the shorthand.

### Named page rules

Define different page settings for sections of the document. The rule name matches the section div class (minus the `section-` prefix).

```css
/* Default page settings */
@page { size: letter; margin: 1in; }

/* Landscape section for wide tables */
@page landscape { size: letter landscape; margin: 0.5in; }

/* Body section with line numbers */
@page body { size: letter; margin: 1in; }
```

**Usage in QMD:**

```markdown
::: {.section-landscape}
Wide table here...
:::

::: {.section-default}
:::
```

### `--docstyle-line-numbers`

Enables line numbering in a page section.

```css
@page body {
  --docstyle-line-numbers: every 1;           /* every N | none */
  --docstyle-line-numbers-restart: continuous; /* continuous | page | section */
  --docstyle-line-numbers-distance: 0.25in;   /* distance from text edge */
}
```

| Property | Values | Default |
|---|---|---|
| `--docstyle-line-numbers` | `every N`, `none` | `none` |
| `--docstyle-line-numbers-restart` | `page`, `section`, `continuous` | `page` |
| `--docstyle-line-numbers-distance` | CSS length | `0.25in` |

### `--docstyle-suppress-top-spacing`

Suppresses the before-spacing on the first content paragraph after a section break. This compensates for Word not honouring `suppressSpBfAfterPgBrk` after section breaks.

```css
@page { --docstyle-suppress-top-spacing: true; }  /* global default */
@page body { --docstyle-suppress-top-spacing: true; }  /* per-section */
```

Can be overridden per section div:

```markdown
::: {.section-body suppress-top-spacing="true"}
## Introduction
Content here — no top gap on the heading.
:::
```

**Resolution precedence:** div attribute > named `@page` > global `@page`.

## Table properties

Tables use CSS-driven styling via `::: {.table-*}` div fences. Define table classes in CSS, then apply them in QMD.

### Border properties

```css
/* Top and bottom borders only (formal style) */
.table-formal {
  border-top: 1pt solid #000000;
  border-bottom: 1pt solid #000000;
}

/* All borders (grid style) */
.table-grid {
  border: 1pt solid #000000;
}

/* Cell-level borders */
.table-grid th, .table-grid td {
  border: 1pt solid #000000;
}
```

**Border format:** `width style color`

| CSS style | OOXML style |
|---|---|
| `solid` | `single` |
| `dashed` | `dashed` |
| `dotted` | `dotted` |
| `double` | `double` |
| `none` | (no border) |

**Unit:** Eighth-points (1pt = 8 eighth-points).

### Header styling

```css
.table-formal th {
  background-color: #D9D9D9;
  font-weight: bold;
}
```

**OOXML output:**

```xml
<w:shd w:val="clear" w:color="auto" w:fill="D9D9D9"/>
<w:b/>
```

### Font size

```css
.table-formal { font-size: 9pt; }
```

Sets the font size for all text in the table.

### Div attribute overrides

Div attributes override CSS defaults for individual tables:

```markdown
::: {.table-formal widths="30,70" font-size="9" width="80"}
| Narrow | Wide column |
|--------|-------------|
| Data   | More data   |
:::
```

| Attribute | Effect | Example |
|---|---|---|
| `widths` | Column width percentages (comma-separated) | `widths="25,50,25"` |
| `font-size` | Font size in points | `font-size="9"` |
| `width` | Table width as % of page | `width="80"` |
| `header-bold` | Override header bold | `header-bold="false"` |
| `header-shading` | Override header background colour | `header-shading="#E8E8E8"` |

## CSS selectors

### Selector-to-style mapping

CSS selectors map to Word style IDs. Multiple selectors can target the same style.

**Headings:**

| CSS Selector | Word Style |
|---|---|
| `h1` | Heading 1 |
| `h2` | Heading 2 |
| `h3` | Heading 3 |
| `h4` | Heading 4 |
| `h5` | Heading 5 |
| `h6` | Heading 6 |
| `h7`–`h9` | Heading 7–9 |

**Body text:**

| CSS Selector | Word Style |
|---|---|
| `p`, `body` | Normal |
| `.body-text` | Body Text |
| `.first-paragraph` | First Paragraph |
| `.compact` | Compact |
| `blockquote`, `.blockquote` | Block Text |
| `code`, `.code` | Source Code |

**Title page:**

| CSS Selector | Word Style |
|---|---|
| `.title` | Title |
| `.subtitle` | Subtitle |
| `.author` | Author |
| `.affiliation` | Affiliation |
| `.date` | Date |
| `.version` | Version |
| `.abstract` | Abstract |
| `.abstract-title` | Abstract Title |

**Captions and figures:**

| CSS Selector | Word Style |
|---|---|
| `caption`, `.caption` | Caption |
| `.table-caption` | Table Caption |
| `.image-caption` | Image Caption |
| `.figure` | Figure |
| `.captioned-figure` | Captioned Figure |

**Definitions:**

| CSS Selector | Word Style |
|---|---|
| `dt`, `.definition-term` | Definition Term |
| `dd`, `.definition` | Definition |

**References:**

| CSS Selector | Word Style |
|---|---|
| `.bibliography`, `.references` | Bibliography |
| `.footnote-text` | Footnote Text |

**Table of contents:**

| CSS Selector | Word Style |
|---|---|
| `.toc-heading` | TOC Heading |
| `.toc-1` through `.toc-5` | TOC 1–5 |

**Headers and footers:**

| CSS Selector | Word Style |
|---|---|
| `.header` | Header |
| `.footer` | Footer |

**Custom classes:** Any `.name` selector not in the list above creates a new Word paragraph style with that name.

```css
.callout { font-style: italic; color: #666666; }
```

### Style cascade

CSS on `p`/`body` (mapped to Word's Normal style) cascades to child styles automatically. You only need explicit rules when overriding the parent.

```
Normal
├── Body Text
│   ├── First Paragraph
│   ├── Compact
│   └── Block Text
├── Heading 1–9
├── Title
│   └── Subtitle
├── Footnote Text
├── Bibliography
├── Definition Term / Definition
└── Caption
    ├── Table Caption
    └── Image Caption
```

**Example:** Setting `p { font-size: 12pt; }` applies 12pt to Normal, which cascades to Body Text, First Paragraph, Compact, headings, and all other child styles — unless they have their own CSS rule.

This cascade prevents Pandoc from overwriting child styles with its hardcoded defaults.

## Unit conversions

docstyle converts CSS units to Word's internal units during pre-render.

### Supported units

| CSS Unit | Base Conversion | Example |
|---|---|---|
| `pt` | 1pt (base) | `12pt` |
| `px` | 1px = 0.75pt | `16px` = 12pt |
| `in` | 1in = 72pt | `1in` = 72pt |
| `cm` | 1cm = 28.35pt | `2.54cm` = 72pt |
| `mm` | 1mm = 2.835pt | `25.4mm` = 72pt |
| `pc` | 1pc = 12pt | `1pc` = 12pt |
| `em` | 1em = base font size | `1.5em` = 18pt (at 12pt base) |
| `rem` | Same as `em` | `1.5rem` = 18pt (at 12pt base) |
| `%` | Percentage of base | `150%` = 18pt (at 12pt base) |

Default base font size for `em`/`rem`/`%`: 12pt.

### OOXML unit systems

| OOXML Context | Unit Name | Conversion |
|---|---|---|
| Font sizes (`w:sz`) | Half-points | points × 2 |
| Margins, spacing, indentation | Twips | points × 20 |
| Borders (`w:sz` on borders) | Eighth-points | points × 8 |
| Page dimensions (`w:pgSz`) | Twips | points × 20 |

**Common conversions:**

| CSS | Twips | Half-points | Eighth-points |
|---|---|---|---|
| `1in` | 1440 | — | — |
| `1cm` | 567 | — | — |
| `12pt` | 240 | 24 | 96 |
| `1pt` | 20 | 2 | 8 |
| `0.5pt` | 10 | 1 | 4 |

## Unsupported properties

The following CSS properties have no Word equivalent and are silently ignored:

- `background-image`, `background-position`, `background-repeat`
- `box-shadow`, `text-shadow`
- `display`, `position`, `float`, `flex`, `grid`
- `width`, `height`, `max-width`, `min-height` (on non-table elements)
- `border-radius`
- `opacity`
- `transition`, `animation`
- `@media` queries
- `::before`, `::after` pseudo-elements
- `:hover`, `:focus` pseudo-classes

**Partial support:**

- `line-height`: Only numeric multipliers (e.g., `1.5`). Unit-based values (e.g., `18pt`) produce a warning and are ignored.
- `border-collapse`: Parsed but not used — table layout is always fixed.

## See also

- [YAML configuration reference](reference-yaml-schema.html): All `docstyle:` configuration options
- [File conventions](reference-file-conventions.html): Sidecar files and directory structure
- [Creating Word templates from CSS](css-to-word.html): Tutorial on CSS-to-Word workflow
- [Architecture](architecture.html): How docstyle processes CSS through the pipeline
